agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A logistics platform managing parcel delivery lifecycle — from shipment creation through transit to final delivery — with weight-based pricing tiers.","bounded_context":"Shipment Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Shipment","definition":"A parcel delivery unit tracking the movement of goods from a pickup address to a delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states."},{"term":"Pickup Address","definition":"The origin location where the parcel is collected."},{"term":"Delivery Address","definition":"The destination location where the parcel is to be delivered."},{"term":"Pending","definition":"Initial shipment state after creation, before transit begins."},{"term":"In Transit","definition":"Shipment state indicating the parcel is actively being transported. Irreversible back to Pending."},{"term":"Delivered","definition":"Terminal shipment state indicating the parcel has reached its destination. No further state changes allowed."},{"term":"Weight Bracket","definition":"A pricing classification determined by shipment weight: Standard (<10 kg), Heavy (≥10 kg and <50 kg), or Freight (≥50 kg)."},{"term":"Standard","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments under 10 kg."},{"term":"Heavy","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing 10 kg up to but not including 50 kg."},{"term":"Freight","definition":"Weight bracket for shipments weighing 50 kg or above, up to the 1000 kg maximum."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"A shipment requires both a pickup address and a delivery address at creation time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment without both endpoints is physically meaningless — there is no valid delivery without an origin and a destination. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create a shipment missing either pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"A shipment in the In Transit state cannot transition back to Pending.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Reversing transit contradicts physical reality — a parcel that has left the origin cannot be un-dispatched. Allowing this would corrupt shipment tracking integrity.","testable_assertion":"Given a shipment in In Transit state, any request to change its state to Pending must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A Delivered shipment is in a terminal state — no further state transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Delivery is a physical finality. Reopening a delivered shipment would corrupt audit trails, billing records, and customer communications. State integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Given a shipment in Delivered state, any request to change its state to any other state must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Shipment weight must be strictly positive (greater than zero).","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A shipment with zero or negative weight is physically impossible and would corrupt weight-bracket pricing calculations.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create or update a shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 1000 kg cap is a business policy limit — likely tied to vehicle capacity or insurance — that a VP could revise. It is not a physical or data-integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to create or update a shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Weight bracket Standard applies when weight is greater than 0 and less than 10 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing brackets are business policy, adjustable by commercial teams without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight in (0, 10) must be classified as Standard."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Weight bracket Heavy applies when weight is greater than or equal to 10 kg and less than 50 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing brackets are business policy.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight in [10, 50) must be classified as Heavy."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Weight bracket Freight applies when weight is greater than or equal to 50 kg and up to the maximum of 1000 kg.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing brackets are business policy.","testable_assertion":"A shipment with weight in [50, 1000] must be classified as Freight."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"The valid shipment state transitions are: Pending → In Transit → Delivered. No other transitions are permitted.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"The state machine encodes the physical lifecycle of a parcel. Arbitrary transitions would corrupt tracking, billing, and audit integrity.","testable_assertion":"Only the transitions Pending→In Transit and In Transit→Delivered are accepted; all others are rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A valid pickup address and delivery address and a positive weight not exceeding 1000 kg","when":"A shipment is created","then":"The shipment is created in Pending state with the correct weight bracket assigned"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A missing pickup address or a missing delivery address","when":"A shipment creation is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"The shipment is moved to In Transit","then":"The shipment state changes to In Transit"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"An attempt is made to change the state back to Pending","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains In Transit"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A shipment in In Transit state","when":"The shipment is moved to Delivered","then":"The shipment state changes to Delivered"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A shipment in Delivered state","when":"An attempt is made to change the state to any other state","then":"The system rejects the transition and the shipment remains Delivered"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A shipment with weight 0 or negative","when":"A shipment creation or update is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A shipment with weight exceeding 1000 kg","when":"A shipment creation or update is attempted","then":"The system rejects the request with a validation error"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A shipment with weight 5 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The bracket is Standard"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 10 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The bracket is Heavy"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"A shipment with weight 25 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The bracket is Heavy"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A shipment with weight exactly 50 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The bracket is Freight"},{"id":"AC-13","given":"A shipment with weight 1000 kg","when":"The weight bracket is calculated","then":"The bracket is Freight"},{"id":"AC-14","given":"A shipment in Pending state","when":"An attempt is made to transition directly to Delivered","then":"The system rejects the transition — shipments must pass through In Transit first"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Weight exactly at 10 kg boundary","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy (10 kg is the inclusive lower bound of the Heavy bracket)"},{"scenario":"Weight at 9.999... kg (just below 10 kg)","expected_behavior":"Classified as Standard"},{"scenario":"Weight exactly at 50 kg boundary","expected_behavior":"Classified as Freight (50 kg is the inclusive lower bound of the Freight bracket)"},{"scenario":"Weight at 49.999... kg (just below 50 kg)","expected_behavior":"Classified as Heavy"},{"scenario":"Weight exactly at 1000 kg (maximum)","expected_behavior":"Accepted and classified as Freight"},{"scenario":"Weight at 1000.001 kg (just over maximum)","expected_behavior":"Rejected — exceeds maximum allowed weight"},{"scenario":"Attempt to transition Pending directly to Delivered (skipping In Transit)","expected_behavior":"Rejected — the state machine requires Pending → In Transit → Delivered in sequence"},{"scenario":"Attempt to create shipment with weight of exactly 0","expected_behavior":"Rejected — weight must be strictly positive"},{"scenario":"Attempt to re-deliver an already Delivered shipment","expected_behavior":"Rejected — Delivered is a terminal state, no transitions permitted"}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct weight bracket classification directly determines pricing. Misclassification (e.g., charging Standard rate for a 50 kg Freight parcel) causes revenue leakage. Enforcing the 1000 kg cap prevents accepting shipments that exceed operational capacity.","operational":"The strict state machine (Pending → In Transit → Delivered) ensures reliable shipment tracking, prevents logistics confusion from state reversals, and maintains clean audit trails for operations teams.","user":"Customers rely on accurate state progression for delivery visibility. Terminal Delivered state gives confidence that shipment tracking is trustworthy. Clear weight limits set correct expectations at booking time.","risk":"Primary risks are boundary misclassification at 10 kg and 50 kg thresholds (revenue leakage) and allowing illegal state transitions (data corruption, audit trail unreliability). Both are mitigatable with strict validation.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Accurate pricing and reliable shipment tracking. Incorrect brackets lead to billing disputes; illegal state transitions erode trust in delivery status."},{"group":"Operations / Dispatch","impact":"Clean state machine ensures dispatchers can trust shipment status. No phantom reversals or impossible states to debug."},{"group":"Finance / Billing","impact":"Weight bracket correctness directly drives revenue accuracy. Boundary errors compound across high shipment volumes."},{"group":"Warehouse / Logistics","impact":"1000 kg cap ensures shipments fit within handling and vehicle capacity constraints."}]},"confidence":{"level":92,"reasoning":"The product manager's spec is unusually precise — explicit boundary inclusivity for weight brackets, clear state transition rules, and unambiguous terminal state definition. Very little is left to interpretation.","high_confidence_areas":["State machine transitions and terminal state","Weight bracket boundaries with explicit inclusivity","Shipment creation prerequisites","Weight validation constraints"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether weight can be fractional (e.g., 10.5 kg) or must be integer — spec does not state precision","Whether Pending → Delivered skip is explicitly forbidden or just implied by the two stated rules","Whether weight can be modified after creation or only set at creation time"],"assumptions":["Weight is a continuous positive number (fractional kg allowed)","Pending → Delivered directly is forbidden — only sequential transitions through In Transit are valid","The state machine has exactly three states: Pending, In Transit, Delivered","Weight bracket assignment happens at creation time based on weight"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this logistics domain description and produce the full domain analysis JSON.

The rules are clear:
1. Shipment creation requires pickup and delivery addresses
2. State transitions: pending -> in_transit -> delivered (no back to pending from in_transit, delivered is final)
3. Weight: positive, max 1000kg
4. Weight brackets: <10kg standard, 10-49.99kg heavy, >=50kg freight

Let me produce the JSON output.
